title: ADP clips part (layer 3)
description: The package's clips part: an array of first-class clip objects. A clip is a layer-3 object that PLACES a piece of footage on the canonical UTC timeline (a time range) and references the video asset it is cut from — it is NOT a copy of the footage. Sport-neutral: nothing here is MTB-specific. Producer video/segment blocks (e.g. rfr's manifest.clips[]) map onto this canonical shape; a consumer can rebuild its own clip list from the light core without re-probing the heavy video. Distinct from enrichment.schema $defs/clip, which models user-authored highlight/annotation clips (possibly multi-range) anchored to the timeline; a clip here is one contiguous footage segment tied to a video asset.

clip.title: Clip
clip.description: One footage segment placed on the canonical timeline. Required core: where it sits on the timeline (start_utc/end_utc/duration_s), the video asset it references (video_ref), and the video↔telemetry sync offset (offset_s).

clip_id.description: Optional stable id of this clip within the package.

video_ref.description: Reference to the video asset this clip is cut from: the `part` name of a role:"video" part in the manifest (NOT embedded footage). A consumer resolves it to the actual media via that part's location.

start_utc.description: Start of the clip's range on the canonical UTC timeline (= recording_start_utc shifted by offset_s).

end_utc.description: End of the clip's range on the canonical UTC timeline.

duration_s.description: Footage duration in seconds. Carried explicitly (not derived from end-start) because producers may report a rounded/independent duration.

start_local.description: OPTIONAL local-time mirror of start_utc (ISO 8601 with offset), a producer convenience derivable from start_utc + the package timezone. Carried when the producer supplies it so consumer views round-trip faithfully.

offset_s.description: Video↔telemetry sync offset in seconds: the per-footage alignment of the camera clock onto the canonical timeline (start_utc = recording_start_utc + offset_s). Lives ON THE CLIP because it is per-footage, not a per-source clock property. 0 = camera already aligned.

direction.description: OPTIONAL playback direction of the footage relative to the timeline. live-trails uses it (POV forward/reverse); most producers do not. Omit when not applicable.

intent.description: OPTIONAL free-text reason the clip exists (e.g. 'full descent', 'rock garden').

derived_metrics.description: OPTIONAL convenience aggregates computed over the clip's range (open set, e.g. max_speed_ms, avg_hr_bpm).

video.description: Technical description of the referenced footage, generalized from producer video metadata. Sport-neutral; every field optional so audio-only or non-video sources degrade gracefully.

file.description: Original file name of the footage (the layer-1 artifact video_ref points at).

recording_start_utc.description: When the camera started recording (UTC). Distinct from start_utc: start_utc = recording_start_utc + offset_s.
